Notes: Back cover for Drake's second mixtape "Comeback Season".

Name: So Far Gone
Notes:
The cover art for the So Far Gone mixtape features a silhouette of a child (representing the purer stage of life before the corruptions of growing up and becoming an adult/man) standing on top of white bold letters that spell out “DRAKE. SO FAR GONE AN OCTOBER’S VERY OWN PRESENTATION” with money cascading down. The EP’s cover has a black background with “DRAKE SO FAR GONE” in bold, white letters, and red hearts and money spilling from above. The reason for the child, red hearts and red money can be found in the following quote,

"One night we were having a discussion about women and the way we were talking about them. It was so brazen and so disrespectful. He texted me right after we got off the phone and he was like, ‘Are we becoming the men that our mothers divorced?’ That’s really where the cover comes from, too. It’s just this kid in pursuit of love and money."

Name: So Far Gone (5 Year Anniversary)
Notes: Cover of Drake's third mixtape So Far Gone, made for the five year anniversary.

Name: So Far Gone (10 Year Anniversary)
Notes: Drake used this image in a post announcing the streaming re-release of So Far Gone in honor of its ten-year anniversary. In the original cover, the silhouette of a boy is seen reaching upwards towards the wealth and love that are falling down upon him. However, this image portrays the boy now looking down on these things, as he already indulged in them. The updated artwork is a representation of Drake’s career. At the time of the tape’s original 2009 release, Drake was barely starting to become a household name. Ten years later, Drake is arguably the biggest artist in the entire world and a modern legend of the music industry. Also, the silhouette in this image only wields money, which could allude to Drake having prioritized attaining wealth over seeking love. Although Drake posted this cover, it was not officially attached to the tape’s re-release.

Name: So Far Gone [Back Cover]
Notes: The back cover of So Far Gone in the same style of the mixtape’s cover art.

Name: Thank Me Later
Notes:
This cover art was made by Darkie who also designed the So Far Gone mixtape cover art. As the debut studio album for Drake, the simple bold design featuring Drake’s face and title serve the purpose of introducing the start of Drake’s career and self-branding with style and clarity. When speaking of the creation Darkie said the following,

"Thank Me Later was totally different. At this point, Oliver and I had a pretty comfortable working relationship. He approached me regarding doing the album artwork and, of course, I was down. He must have had this one planned out for a while ‘cause he had pulled a mood board that was the exact vibe of the theme, which was a play on Hezbollah’s propaganda war. It was nuts, but ill at the same time."

Name: Take Care
Notes: The imagery here is that of conflicting thematic moods; as the Toronto rapper finds himself draped in lavish gold within a luxury setting, but is immensely sombre in stance and expression. An unfamiliar pairing in hip-hop, as many artists in the genre aspire to attain this level of wealth and when they do, they tend to flaunt it in a braggadocios fashion. This cover was shot by one of XO’s founders, Hyghly Alleyne, who also did the cover art for Views and shot the videos for “Marvin’s Room,” “Headlines” and “The Motto.”

Name: Nothing Was The Same
Notes: The album cover for Nothing Was The Same was originally an oil painting by Kadir Nelson, inspired by Drake's music. Kadir created sketches, picked the best one, and sculpted it together. He also gave Drake a full size painting of the cover. The cover was inspired by Kadir's painting, Cleaned Cotton. It shows a young Drake with an afro comb and an older Drake with a Gold chain. The cover is similar to Nas' covers for Illmatic and It Was Written, representing Drake's journey in the music industry and his life. Drake discussed the cover on CRWN, expressing his admiration for its soulful and powerful imagery. He was inspired by Kadir's work and reached out to him to collaborate on the album cover.

The cover is similar to Nas' covers for Illmatic and It Was Written, representing Drake's journey in the music industry and his life. Drake discussed the cover on CRWN, expressing his admiration for its soulful and powerful imagery. He was inspired by Kadir's work and reached out to him to collaborate on the album cover.

Name: If You're Reading This It's Too Late
Notes: NYC street artist Jim Joe created the mixtape art for If You're Reading This It's Too Late with the mixtape's title written sloppy handwriting to resemble a ransom letter, possibly alluding to Drake's issues with his label. The title, "If you're reading this, it's too late" takes a dig at Birdman, referencing Lil Wayne's feud with his former mentor. Drake released the mixtape as an album to free himself from his record contract, indicating his split from Cash Money. Wayne and Drake are now working together to save Young Money.

Name: Scorpion
Notes:
Drake unveiled the cover of his album on June 14, 2018 with an Instagram post. The Scorpion cover is the fifth consecutive Drake studio album cover to feature his likeness (NWTS’s deluxe edition is Drake as an adult and the standard is him as a kid, and Views has Drake photoshopped to be sitting on Toronto’s CN Tower).

The cover also features a signature consisting of Drake’s initials: A. G. for Aubrey Graham. Drake also used the album to confirm the speculation about having fathered a son named Adonis, whose initials are also A. G.

The album art was designed by Julian Consuegra, owner and founder of the streetwear brand Stray Rats. For unknown reasons, the cover is very compressed even at higher resolutions.

Name: Scorpion [Back Cover]
Notes:
Just a few hours before the release of Scorpion, Drake uploaded this photo of the tracklist through his Instagram. Scorpion was revealed to be a double album, meaning the “A SIDE” will contain 12 tracks, and “B SIDE” would contain 13.

In mid-June 2018, no more than two weeks before the album’s release, multiple billboards across Toronto reading “Side A/Side B” was the first hint to fans that Drake would be releasing a double-album.

Name: Certified Lover Boy
Notes:
The main cover for Certified Lover Boy, which was designed by English artist Damien Hirst, consists of 12 pregnant women Apple emojis. It was first shown on August 30th on Drake’s Instagram page, accompanied by a caption confirming the album’s release date: September 3rd.

The reviews for this cover art (alongside it’s merch drop in partnership with Nike) have been mainly negative since its release, calling it “lazy and childish.”

It can also be noted that the cover art pays homage to Damien Hirst as the CLB cover is a combination of two Damien Hirst artworks. The Virgin Mother (2005) and Spot Paintings (2012). Furthermore the album has been recognised in being 9 months behind on its release – the same duration pregnancy is expected to be.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Animated Cover]
Notes: Animated cover for Drake's "Certified Lover Boy" for streaming services.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Back Cover]
Notes:
This is the tracklist of Drake’s sixth studio album, Certified Lover Boy. It was revealed on social media on September 2, 2021.

The tracklist is written in dry-erase marker on a whiteboard, likely the one Drake’s son, Adonis, was seen holding in an Instagram post from May 28, 2021, in which Drake teased the album’s tracklist. The CLB logo and first track, “Champagne Poetry,” is identical to the faded versions seen in the Instagram post.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Rough Draft Cover 9]
Notes: Alternate cover of Certified Lover Boy shared by Drake on instagram. Uses the same photo of Drake with model Hayley Karrina as a cover leaked sometime before the album released, except in color and without any title text. It has been speculated the model chosen was intended to resemble Kim Kardashian, a subtle jab at Kanye West.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Rough Draft Cover 10]
Notes: A photo of this cover leaked sometime before the official art for Certified Lover Boy was officially revealed, prior to August 2021. At the time it was considered to be fake, but when Drake shared the same photo used in this cover later after the album's release it seemingly confirmed this was a cover being considered at one point. Only a low quality photo of the cover, seemingly on a print out or computer screen, has surfaced. Given the lack of the heart haircut, this was likely shot sometime before May 2021.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Rough Draft Cover 11]
Notes: Alternate cover of Certified Lover Boy shared by Drake on instagram. Of all the alternate covers shared, this is the only one featuring Drake in which he actually has the distinctive heart outline shaved in his hair. Likely made sometime after May 2021 given the presence of the heart outline haircut.

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Rough Draft Cover 12]
Notes: Alternate cover of Certified Lover Boy shared by Drake on instagram. Seemingly a lipstick stain on a bandage of some sort. Maybe from Drake's infamous ACL injury?

Name: Certified Lover Boy [Rough Draft Cover 13]
Notes: Alternate cover of Certified Lover Boy. Seemingly a work in progress version of the final cover art, possibly not an actual alternate cover itself.

Name: Scary Hours II
Notes: Cover for "Scary Hours 2". Features two blue diamonds in the centre of a dark blue background with "Scary Hours" at the top.

Name: Scary Hours II [Back Cover]

Name: Honestly, Nevermind
Notes: Revealed only seven hours before the release of the record, the cover art for Honestly, Nevermind is a diamond encrusted, cursive text of the album’s title, featuring bright colors beneath it. It was designed by French artist, Ben Dorado. The pitch black background reflects the album’s sound – deep, dark, melodic house music.

Notes: The album’s 14-track list includes one omitted feature (21 Savage on Jimmy Cooks) and the usual OVO crew along with Black Coffee helping to put this body of work together.

Name: Her Loss
Notes:
Drake announced the cover art for Her Loss on Instagram a day before the release of the album: "Her Loss 🦉🔪 Front Cover"

Shot by Parisaden and edited by Aris Tatalovich, the artwork depicts model and 3D artist Qui Yasuka (or Suki Baby). It was taken for an interview with gtalks! radio.

One day after the release of the album, Lil Yachty stated that the album cover was picked by him: "I Chose This Cover Because This Photo Is So Raw… So Authentic.. Not Fabricated.. Suki Can And Will Only Be Suki… MY BROTHRRR @aristatalovich BROUGHT IT TO LIFE…. HER LOSS 🦉🗡️ FUCK WHO AINT WIT US – CVO"

Notes: Animated cover for Drake & 21 Savage's "Her Loss".

Name: Her Loss [Back Cover]

Name: For All The Dogs
Notes: This is the cover artwork for Drake’s eighth studio album, For All The Dogs. The art was revealed in an August 2023 Instagram post, featuring a goat, drawn by Drake's son, Adonis Graham. In the music video for 8am In Charlotte, Adonis explains the artwork depicts a story of a goat running from monsters and a person getting killed by a trap, symbolizing Drake as the G.O.A.T.

Name: For All The Dogs [Back Cover]
Notes: For All The Dogs‘ back cover, showcasing its official tracklist, was revealed by Drake just hours prior to the album’s release. The artwork itself is once again courtesy of Drake’s son Adonis Graham, who also drew the front cover. The “8am In Charlotte” music video displays Adonis' full art-piece that was fragmented into FOR ALL THE DOGS‘ front cover and back cover.

Name: For All The Dogs [Rough Draft Cover 1]
Notes: Unused cover for "For All The Dogs" found in "100 gigs for your headtop". Visually similar to the design draw by Adonis for the official cover, with a dog in the middle on a blank background, except in this case it's a black dog on a white background.

Name: For All The Dogs [Rough Draft Cover 2]
Notes: Unused cover for "For All The Dogs" found in "100 gigs for your headtop". Tecnichally not the original cover, as the file included is a screenshot and may be innacurately cropped and lower quality than a proper export of the art.

Name: For All The Dogs: Scary Hours Edition
Notes: The cover for For All The Dogs: Scary Hours Edition, designed by Ben Dorado, features two fairies holding each other’s hands, posted to his social media the night before the EP’s release. In the post, he tagged the producers featured on the album such as Conductor Williams, The Alchemist, Boi-1da, Ovrkast, and Vinylz. This cover is also color coded similar to the key art for Drake’s It’s All A Blur Tour promotion posters.
Designer:
Roger Schami,
Marvin Lau,
Joshuisms,
Kxvn

Name: Search & Rescue
Notes: On April 6, 2023, Drake announced Search & Rescue’s official release date with the cover art. The artwork contains a superimposed image of Drake cozying up with UK vocalist Lilah, a woman that bares a striking resemblance to his mentor-turned-rival Kanye West’s ex-wife, Kim Kardashian, whilst sporting black helmets. Notably, the song also samples Kardashian and her mom, Kris Jenner, discussing Kim’s divorce from Ye, lifted from an episode of Keeping Up with the Kardashians.

Name: Push Ups
Notes: Cover art for "Push Ups", the cover is mocking Kendrick's shoe size.

Name: THE HEART PART 6
Notes: For the cover art for "THE HEART PART 6", Drake uses a screenshot of an Instagram comment from Dave Free, Kendrick Lamar's business partner and friend, as the artwork for his song. The comment was left on a post from Whitney Alford, Kendrick's fiancé, featuring their two kids. This is likely a taunt from Drake, referencing his previous diss track "Family Matters" where he implied Free might be the father of one of Kendrick's children.
